summary: Matched Peach's ftPeach_OnLoad by replacing the handwritten assembly with C, adding enough Fighter and ftData layout information to access ftData, special attribute pointers, and item pointers, and declaring three helper functions used by the on-load routine. The implementation loads two float attributes through func_80085E50/func_8001E8F8, copies Peach extended attributes into the fighter's special-attributes buffer, updates x2D4_specialAttributes to point at that copied buffer, and registers five item pointers with IDs 98, 99, 103, 104, and 111 via func_8026B3F8.
what_changed: Removed the ftPeach_OnLoad assembly body from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftpeach.s.
Added a local PairStruct in ftpeach.c containing two f32 fields followed by 0xB8 bytes of padding, allowing a whole-struct assignment to reproduce the 0xC0-byte copy loop from assembly.
Expanded Fighter in src/melee/ft/fighter.h with x0_fighter, x10C_ftData, x2D4_specialAttributes, x2D8_specialAttributes2, and adjusted filler ranges to preserve known offsets.
Introduced ftData in fighter.h with fields including common_attr at 0x00, ext_attr at 0x04, and items at 0x48.
Added typed declarations in include/functions.h for func_8001E8F8, func_80085E50, and func_8026B3F8.

ftPeach_OnLoad now pulls Fighter from gobj->user_data, accesses player->x10C_ftData, writes ext_attr float slots 0 and 4, copies ext_attr to player->x2D8_specialAttributes2, stores that destination pointer into x2D4_specialAttributes, and registers five items from ftDataInfo->items.
smart_moves: Used a temporary PairStruct with exact size implied by the assembly copy loop, converting a 0x18-iteration pairwise word copy into a simple C struct assignment.

Modeled the relevant Fighter offsets explicitly instead of relying on one large opaque filler, which made x10C_ftData, x2D4_specialAttributes, and x2D8_specialAttributes2 usable for future fighter work.
Kept unknown structure fields conservatively named with offset-based placeholders rather than overclaiming semantics.
Promoted only the required helper prototypes from unknown comments to typed declarations, enough to type-check this match without broad unrelated header churn.
Preserved the item registration sequence directly from the assembly constants: items[0..4] mapped to IDs 98, 99, 103, 104, and 111.
decomp_lessons: For character OnLoad functions, check whether the assembly is mostly attribute initialization, special-attribute copying, and item registration; these patterns can often be matched with simple pointer fields plus exact-size local structs.
When assembly copies a fixed byte range with a counted loop, a same-sized struct assignment can be a compact matching tactic if the compiler emits equivalent copy code.
Adding known offsets to shared structs such as Fighter can unblock many future matches, but filler arrays must be recalculated carefully around each newly exposed field.
When the underlying data layout is not fully known, offset-based names such as x10C_ftData and x2D8_specialAttributes2 are safer than speculative semantic names.

Function prototypes can be inferred narrowly from call sites: func_80085E50 returns a u32 token used as the argument to func_8001E8F8, which returns f32; func_8026B3F8 takes an item pointer and an integer ID.
naming_conventions: Used offset-prefixed field names for partially understood Fighter fields: x10C_ftData, x2D4_specialAttributes, x2D8_specialAttributes2, x618_flag, x222C.
Named the new fighter data struct ftData and the local Peach attribute-copy helper PairStruct.
Kept unknown ftData members as offset-style names such as x08, x0C, x10, x44, x4C, and x58.

Used descriptive local variables in ftPeach_OnLoad: player, ftDataInfo, extAtrrs, items, dest, src, and attr. The spelling extAtrrs appears in the diff and should be treated as evidence from this PR, not as a recommended spelling.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Translated gobj->user_data from the assembly load at offset 0x2C into Fighter* player = gobj->user_data.
Matched loads through Fighter offset 0x10C and ftData offsets 0x04 and 0x48 by adding ftData* x10C_ftData, ext_attr, and items fields.
Translated the two calls using IDs 0x12 and 0x13 into extAtrrs->unk0 = func_8001E8F8(func_80085E50(player, 18)) and extAtrrs->unk4 = func_8001E8F8(func_80085E50(player, 19)).
Replaced the assembly loop copying 0x18 pairs of words from ext_attr to x2D8_specialAttributes2 with *dest = *src, using PairStruct size 0xC0.
Reproduced the post-copy pointer update by writing the destination buffer address into player->x2D4_specialAttributes.
Kept item registration call order and constants exactly aligned with the assembly: 0x62, 0x63, 0x67, 0x68, and 0x6F.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that renamed or refined ftData fields such as ext_attr, items, x2D4_specialAttributes, or x2D8_specialAttributes2.
Search for other fighter OnLoad matches that use func_80085E50 and func_8001E8F8 for special attribute initialization.
Look for later corrections to the PairStruct size or replacement with a named Peach special attributes struct.
Search for item registration patterns using func_8026B3F8 with character-specific item IDs.
